reasoning: FIDE ratings natively update only on the 1st of every month. The Wikipedia 'FIDE rankings' page reflects these monthly standard rating lists. The rating on November 16 will use the identically same monthly list (from Nov 1) as the rating on November 9, so a 1% increase is functionally impossible, leading to the floor probability for the first date. For the subsequent dates (December and February lists), Lei Tingjie would need an enormous gain of approximately 26 Elo points. For a player rated 2566 (making her #3 in the world), gaining 26 points is an exceptionally rare feat requiring a long streak of wins against top grandmasters, equating to an historic performance peak of 2592. Moreover, search shows Lei is not participating in the prominent rating-eligible Grand Swiss and withdrew from the Grand Prix series, meaning she lacks the volume of games necessary to even mathematically attempt such a leap. Thus, the probability remains exceptionally low across all horizons, rising only slightly due to increasing time variance.
